You are a CLEAN-ROOM implementer under the campaign protocol at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md — read it first and comply strictly.
ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source (*.hs) of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk. Do NOT read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md. No web access. You MAY run the reference binary via console/oracle/hs_oracle.sh (or directly with PATH="$PATH:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin"; ALWAYS under timeout, and prefer bounded --prove runs — long unbounded proof searches are forbidden for resource safety), read .spthy example files, and read/write freely inside /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/. No git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction in workspace QUERIES.log; record behavioral facts in BEHAVIOR.md with provenance; append a round section to a report file (fold into BEHAVIOR.md if .md creation is blocked). Comments describe current behavior only. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary.

UNIT D (console/CLI), round 7 — the NON-diff lemma-verdict family. Round 6 closed the --diff taxonomy (LemmaSide/verdict_phrase); this round targets the remaining per-lemma summary phrases in PLAIN batch mode that your LemmaResult still cannot express.
KNOWN CAPTURED FACT to reproduce first: running plain batch --prove on the example theory /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples/csf23-subterms/YellowTest.spthy, the reference's summary lines for its lemmas read:
    <name> ... analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (4 steps)
— a verdict phrase distinct from verified/falsified that your model cannot render.
Then hunt the rest of the family systematically: bounded proofs (--bound=N exhaustion — how does the summary phrase an unfinished-by-bound analysis, and does it differ from the reducible-operators case?), --stop-on-trace variants, lemmas that error during analysis (e.g. induction on non-inductible lemmas, solver failures), [sources] lemmas with remaining open/partial deconstructions (check the summary's sources line phrasing states), anything --prove=<pattern> non-matching produces, and any advisory lines accompanying these states (e.g. a might-be-wrong warning — pin its exact wording, stream, and position). Sweep example theories for further distinct phrases if helpful (grep your own captured outputs, not sources).
Extend LemmaResult/verdict rendering to cover every phrase found, with byte-parity fixtures per verdict (split streams). All existing tests and fixtures stay green.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).
47

1	# BEHAVIOR.md — Unit D: CLI/console text surface
2	
3	Every statement below traces to an oracle observation captured under
4	`workspace/captures/` (file names in `[brackets]`) or to the SPEC. No
5	tamarin-prover internals were read; all behavior derived from black-box probing
6	of `oracle/hs_oracle.sh`.
7	
8	Oracle note: `hs_oracle.sh <arg1> [rest...]` consumes `arg1` as the first
9	token then appends the rest, i.e. it runs `tamarin-prover <arg1> <rest...>`
10	with stderr merged into stdout. A true zero-argument invocation is therefore
11	NOT observable through this oracle (it always passes at least one token); the
12	closest observable is a single empty-string token (see Ambiguous mode).
13	
14	## 1. Top-level shape
15	
16	The binary is a multi-mode command (cmdargs-style). The first token selects a
17	**mode** by unique prefix match against the mode names; otherwise the default
18	(batch) mode is used and the token is treated as an input FILE.
19	
20	Modes enumerated from help output `[help_global]`:
21	- (default / no token) = batch mode — positional `FILES`.
22	- `interactive` — "Start a web-server to construct proofs interactively." — positional `WORKDIR`.
23	- `variants` — "Compute the variants of the intruder rules for DH-exponentiation." — no positional.
24	- `test` — "Self-test the tamarin-prover installation." — positional `FILES`.
25	
26	Mode prefix matching `[mode_prefix_int/_v/_t]`: `int`→interactive, `v`→variants,
27	`t`→test (any unambiguous non-empty prefix works). An empty token matches all
28	three → ambiguous `[noargs]`.
29	
30	A flag appearing BEFORE a would-be mode token keeps the default batch mode; the
31	token then becomes a filename `[flag_before_mode]`: `-v interactive` →
32	default mode, "interactive" opened as a file → openFile error.
33	
34	## 2. Exit codes
35	
36	- `--help` / per-mode `--help`, `--version`/`-V`, successful batch run: exit 0.
37	- Every parse error and every runtime error observed: exit 1.
38	
39	## 3. `--help` (global and per-mode)  [help_global, help_interactive, help_variants, help_test]
40	
41	Each mode's help has the same skeleton:
42	
43	    <mode-usage-line>
44	      <mode-description>.
45	
46	    Commands:
47	      interactive  Start a web-server to construct proofs interactively.
48	      variants     Compute the variants of the intruder rules for DH-exponentiation.
49	      test         Self-test the tamarin-prover installation.
50	
51	    Flags:
52	      <flag rows, two-column, right column wrapped to a fixed width>
53	    About:
54	      <help/version rows>
55	
56	    <blank>
57	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	    To show help for different commands, type tamarin-prover [Command] --help.
59	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	    See 'https://github.com/tamarin-prover/tamarin-prover/blob/master/README.md'
61	    for usage instructions and pointers to examples.
62	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	    <blank>
64	
65	Usage lines:
66	- global: `tamarin-prover [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS] FILES`
67	- interactive: `interactive [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS] WORKDIR`
68	- variants: `variants [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS]`   (no positional)
69	- test: `test [COMMAND] ... [OPTIONS] FILES`
70	
71	The Commands block is IDENTICAL in all four helps. The footer block (7 lines
72	ending with a trailing blank line) is identical in all four. Every help output
73	ends with `----\n\n` (footer rule + one trailing empty line).
74	
75	The right-hand description column width differs by mode because cmdargs sizes
76	the left column to the widest flag in THAT mode: global uses a very wide left
77	column (widest flag `-m --output-module[=...]`), the other three narrower.
78	Because the exact wrapping/padding is compatibility output, the four help texts
79	are stored verbatim as fixtures and emitted as opaque strings — the clean code
80	does not re-derive column math.
81	
82	Availability of About rows:
83	- default/global mode: `-? --help` AND `-V --version`.
84	- interactive / variants / test: only `-? --help` (no `--version`). Confirmed:
85	  `variants --version` → `Unknown flag: --version` `[version_from_mode]`.
86	
87	### Flag inventory & value semantics (from help text)
88	
89	Value markers: `[=X]` = value OPTIONAL; `=FILE` (no brackets) = value shown
90	required (but see note). Defaults appear in the description text.
91	
92	Default/batch mode flags:
93	- `--prove[=LEMMAPREFIX*|LEMMANAME]`     optional value, repeatable
94	- `--lemma[=LEMMAPREFIX*|LEMMANAME]`     optional value, repeatable
95	- `--stop-on-trace[=DFS|BFS|SEQDFS|SORRY|NONE]`  optional, default DFS
96	- `-b --bound[=INT]`                     optional
97	- `--heuristic[=(C|I|O|P|S|c|i|o|p|s|{.})+]`  optional, default 's'
98	- `--partial-evaluation[=SUMMARY|VERBOSE]`   optional
99	- `-D --defines[=STRING]`                optional
100	- `--diff`                               no value
101	- `--quit-on-warning`                    no value
102	- `--auto-sources`                       no value
103	- `--oraclename[=FILE]`                  optional; default './theory_filename.oracle', fallback './oracle'
104	- `--oracle-only`                        no value
105	- `--quiet`                              no value
106	- `-v --verbose`                         no value
107	- `-c --open-chains[=PositiveInteger]`   optional, default 10
108	- `-s --saturation[=PositiveInteger]`    optional, default 5
109	- `-d --derivcheck-timeout[=INT]`        optional, default 5 (0 deactivates)
110	- `--no-reuse`                           no value
111	- `--no-restrictions`                    no value
112	- `--replication-bound[=INT]`            optional
113	- `--no-compress`                        no value
114	- `--parse-only`                         no value
115	- `--precompute-only`                    no value
116	- `-o --output[=FILE]`                   optional
117	- `-O --Output[=DIR]`                    optional
118	- `-m --output-module[=spthytyped|spthy|msr|proverifequiv|proverif|deepsec]`  optional enum
119	- `--output-json=FILE` (alias `--oj`)    value shown required; NO parse error when omitted [oj_alias_noval,outputjson_noval]
120	- `--output-dot=FILE`  (alias `--od`)    value shown required
121	- `--with-dot[=FILE]`                    optional
122	- `--with-json[=FILE]`                   optional
123	- `--with-maude[=FILE]`                  optional
124	- About: `-? --help`, `-V --version`
125	
126	interactive mode adds: `-p --port[=PORT]`, `-i --interface[=INTERFACE]`,
127	`--image-format[=PNG|SVG]` (default SVG), `--debug`, `--no-logging`; plus the
128	proving/output subset (--prove … --with-maude minus the file-output-module
129	flags: no `--no-compress/--parse-only/--precompute-only/-o/-O/-m/--output-json/
130	--output-dot`). About: only `-? --help`.
131	
132	variants mode: only `-O --Output[=DIR]`; About `-? --help`.
133	
134	test mode: only `--with-dot/--with-json/--with-maude`; About `-? --help`.
135	
136	## 4. `--version` and `-V`  [version, short_version]
137	
138	`--version` and `-V` produce identical output (only valid in default mode).
139	The maude readiness check runs concurrently and its lines INTERLEAVE with the
140	version banner in a fixed, reproducible order:
141	
142	    maude tool: '<MAUDE_PATH>'
143	     checking version: <BANNER line 1: tamarin-prover <VER>, (C) <authors>, <YEARS>>
144	    <blank>
145	    This program comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY. It is free software, and you
146	    are welcome to redistribute it according to its LICENSE, see
147	    'https://github.com/tamarin-prover/tamarin-prover/blob/master/LICENSE'.
148	    <blank>
149	    <MAUDE_VER>. OK.
150	     checking installation: OK.
151	    Generated from:
152	    Tamarin version <VER>
153	    Maude version <MAUDE_VER>
154	    Git revision: <GITREV> (with uncommited changes), branch: <BRANCH>
155	    Compiled at: <TIMESTAMP>
156	
157	The banner text (` checking version: ` prefix + tamarin line + warranty block)
158	is emitted between the maude check's "checking version:" and its "<ver>. OK."
159	because the maude subprocess is being awaited. Dynamic/environment slots:
160	`<MAUDE_PATH>` (default 'maude'), `<VER>` (observed 1.13.0), `<YEARS>`
161	(2010-2023), `<MAUDE_VER>` (3.5.1), `<GITREV>` + dirty-suffix + `<BRANCH>`,
162	`<TIMESTAMP>`. These are build/runtime metadata, injected into the template.
163	
164	## 5. maude preamble (framing prelude)
165	
166	Batch/variants runs that CLOSE a theory (need maude) print a 3-line prelude
167	`[batch_nslpk3_default]`:
168	
169	    maude tool: '<MAUDE_PATH>'
170	     checking version: <MAUDE_VER>. OK.
171	     checking installation: OK.
172	
173	`--parse-only` does NOT print the preamble `[batch_nslpk3_parseonly]` (parsing
174	needs no maude). Runtime file-open errors are printed AFTER this preamble.
175	
176	## 6. Batch-mode framing around a processed theory
177	
178	Full default run `[batch_nslpk3_default]`:
179	
180	    <maude preamble (3 lines)>
181	    [Theory <NAME>] Theory loaded
182	    [Theory <NAME>] Theory translated
183	    [Theory <NAME>] Derivation checks started
184	    [Theory <NAME>] Derivation checks ended
185	    [Theory <NAME>] Theory closed
186	    <OPAQUE THEORY PAYLOAD, from `theory <NAME>` through `end`>
187	    <blank>
188	    ==============================================================================
189	    summary of summaries:
190	    <blank>
191	    analyzed: <ABSOLUTE_FILE_PATH>
192	    <blank>
193	      processing time: <T>s
194	      <-- literal 2-space line
195	    <RESULT LINES>
196	    <blank>
197	    ==============================================================================
198	
199	- `====` rule is exactly 78 `=`. Output ends `====\n` (no trailing content).
200	- `--prove` uses the SAME 5 progress phases `[batch_nslpk3_prove]`.
201	- `--parse-only` prints ONLY `[Theory <NAME>] Theory loaded`, then payload; NO
202	  preamble, NO summary block `[batch_nslpk3_parseonly]`.
203	- The "Generated from:" version comment and any `WARNING:` block are part of the
204	  OPAQUE theory payload (inside `theory … end`), not framing.
205	
206	RESULT LINE forms (2-space indent):
207	- `  <lemma> (all-traces): analysis incomplete (<N> steps)`  [default, unselected]
208	- `  <lemma> (all-traces): verified (<N> steps)`             [proved true]  `[batch_nslpk3_prove]`
209	- `  <lemma> (exists-trace): <result> (<N> steps)`           [exists-trace kind]
210	- (form `falsified (<N> steps)` inferred as the counterpart to `verified`; not
211	  directly captured — see LIMITATIONS.)
212	- `  WARNING: <N> wellformedness check failed!`  [when checks failed; note the
213	  singular "check"] `[round2_multiplication_in_rule_lhs]`
214	
215	When a theory has warnings but no lemmas, the WARNING line is the sole result
216	line. Ordering of the WARNING line vs lemma lines when BOTH are present is not
217	observed (LIMITATIONS).
218	
219	`<T>` (processing time) and `<ABSOLUTE_FILE_PATH>` are dynamic slots.
220	
221	## 7. variants mode run  [mode_variants_run]
222	
223	`variants` (no file): maude preamble, then the pretty-printed intruder
224	variant rules (opaque payload), NO summary block.
225	
226	## 8. Error text taxonomy
227	
228	### 8a. cmdargs parse errors — bare one-liner, exit 1, no help:
229	- Unknown long flag `[err_unknown_flag]`:  `Unknown flag: --foobar`
230	- Unknown short flag `[err_unknown_short]`: `Unknown flag: -Z` (note: `-h` is
231	  NOT help; help is `-?`/`--help`) `[short_help]`
232	- Value given to a valueless flag `[help_eq]`: `Unhandled argument to flag, none expected: --help=foo`
233	- Ambiguous mode `[noargs]`: `Ambiguous mode '', could be any of: interactive variants test`
234	
235	### 8b. cmdargs validation errors — message + blank + FULL help of the active mode, exit 1:
236	- No input file (flags otherwise valid) `[err_stopontrace_bad, err_noinput_diff, err_noinput_prove]`:
237	      error: no input files given
238	      <blank>
239	      <global help body>
240	  Envelope = `error: no input files given\n\n` + exact global help.
241	- Bad interactive WORKDIR `[err_two_modes, interactive_two_pos]`:
242	      error: directory '<DIR>' does not exist.
243	      <blank>
244	      <interactive help body>
245	  (`interactive test` → 'test' is the WORKDIR; `interactive aa bb` → names 'bb'.)
246	
247	### 8c. runtime errors — printed AFTER the maude preamble, exit 1:
248	File open (IOException, no CallStack):
249	- missing `[err_missing_file, token_notfile]`:  `tamarin-prover: <path>: openFile: does not exist (No such file or directory)`
250	- unreadable `[err_unreadable]`:                 `tamarin-prover: <path>: openFile: permission denied (Permission denied)`
251	- directory `[err_isdir]`:                       `tamarin-prover: <path>: openFile: inappropriate type (is a directory)`
252

254	- bad bound `[err_bound_nonint]`:
255	      tamarin-prover: bound: invalid bound given
256	      CallStack (from HasCallStack):

258	- bad output module `[err_output_module_bad]`:

263	These runtime strings (incl. the CallStack source path) are compatibility
264	output taken verbatim from the oracle; they are emitted by the runtime layer
265	after parse(), so the clean crate exposes them as render helpers rather than
266	from parse().
267	
268	## 9. LIMITATIONS / unobserved (documented, not guessed in code)
269	- True zero-argument behavior: not observable through this oracle.
270	- (RESOLVED in R5) `falsified` verdict string: directly captured — it is NOT a
271	  bare `falsified` but a kind-dependent `falsified - found trace` (all-traces) /
272	  `falsified - no trace found` (exists-trace). See §12c.
273	- (RESOLVED in R5) WARNING-line vs lemma-line ordering when both present: the
274	  warning section comes first, separated from the lemma lines by a `  ` line,
275	  and gains an advisory second line under `--prove`. See §12a–b.
276	- Whether `--output-json`/`--output-dot` error at output time when value omitted
277	  (only parse-time / --parse-only observed, where no error occurs).
278	- (RESOLVED in R4) Under `--prove`, extra stderr progress lines DO appear after
279	  `Theory closed` — see §10a.
280	- Interactive-mode value validation (`--port`, `--interface`, `--image-format`):
281	  the server starts even on garbage values (`--port=x .` -> exit 124 server run;
282	  `--image-format=BOGUS .` -> server run), so there is NO CLI-level validation
283	  error to reproduce for those flags `[R4 interactive probes]`.
284	
285	## 10. Stream separation (Round 4)  [split_* captures: <label>.out.txt / .err.txt]
286	
287	The Round 1–3 oracle merged streams (`2>&1`). Re-probing with
288	`1>out 2>err` (probes/split_probe.sh) shows a clean, deterministic split. Each
289	stream is byte-complete on its own; the merged-mode interleaving of §4 was purely
290	a flush artifact of combining two FDs.
291	
292	STDOUT carries:
293	- the opaque theory payload(s) `theory … end` `[split_batch_default.out]`,
294	- the whole "summary of summaries" block `[split_batch_default.out]`,
295	- the `--version`/`-V` banner (tamarin line + warranty + `Generated from:` block)
296	  `[split_version.out — banner ONLY; the merged version.tmpl of §4 wrongly bundled
297	  the maude preamble into stdout]`,
298	- every `--help` / per-mode help page `[split_help.out == help_global.txt]`,
299	- the two cmdargs VALIDATION envelopes `error: … \n\n<full help>`
300	  (no-input, bad-workdir) `[split_err_noinput.out == err_noinput_diff.txt,
301	  split_err_workdir.out == err_two_modes.txt]`.
302	
303	STDERR carries:
304	- the 3-line maude preamble (§5) `[split_batch_default.err]`,
305	- the per-theory progress markers `[Theory <name>] <phase>` `[split_batch_default.err]`,
306	- the bare cmdargs one-liners: `Unknown flag`, `Ambiguous mode`,
307	  `Unhandled argument to flag` `[split_err_unknown.err == err_unknown_flag.txt,
308	  split_err_ambig.err == noargs.txt]`,
309	- all runtime errors: file-open failures and the app `error`/CallStack lines
310	  `[split_err_missing.err, split_err_bound.err]`.
311	
312	`--parse-only` STDERR = one `Theory loaded` line per theory, no preamble; STDOUT =
313	concatenated payloads `[split_batch_parseonly.err/out, split_parseonly_multi]`.
314	`variants` STDOUT = variant payload; STDERR = preamble only `[split_variants.*]`.
315	
316	### 10a. Multi-theory framing  [split_batch_multifile.*]
317	- STDERR: preamble once, then each theory's full 5-phase progress block in order.
318	- STDOUT: payloads concatenated directly (payload_k ends `end\n`, payload_{k+1}
319	  begins `theory …` with NO separator), then ONE combined summary listing every
320	  theory. Payloads are printed only for theories NOT written to an output file.
321	
322	Under `--prove`, a theory's stderr progress block gains extra engine lines AFTER
323	`[Theory <name>] Theory closed`, verbatim `[split_batch_prove.err]`:
324	
325	    [Saturating Sources] Step 1 (Max 5)
326	    [Saturating Sources] Done
327	    [Saturating Sources] Step 1 (Max 5)
328	    [Saturating Sources] Done
329	
330	(`Max 5` = the `--saturation` limit.) A plain analyze run emits none of these. The
331	framing model treats these as an opaque per-theory `extra_progress` slot appended
332	to that theory's five closed phases; the engine supplies the exact text.
333	
334	### 10b. summary-of-summaries exact layout  [split_batch_multifile.out, split_batch_output_single.out]
335	The summary section on STDOUT is:
336	
337	    \n                                  <- separates payload from summary (always emitted)
338	    ==============================================================================   (78 '=')
339	    summary of summaries:
340	    <per theory, each preceded by a blank line:>
341	      analyzed: <PATH-as-passed-on-CLI>
342	      <blank>
343	      [  output:          <OUTPUT-PATH>]   <- only when an output file was written
344	        processing time: <T>s
345	      <2-space line "  ">
346	      <result lines, each "  <text>">
347	    <blank>
348	    ==============================================================================
349	    (final newline)
350	
351	Key/value alignment inside the indented block: labels right-padded to the width of
352	`processing time:` (16), then one space, so values start at column 19 →
353	`  output:` + 10 spaces + value; `  processing time: ` + value. `analyzed:` is a
354	separate un-indented header line with a single space. `output:` is the label for
355	BOTH `-o FILE` and `-O DIR` (the latter writes `<inputstem>_analyzed.spthy`).
356	`analyzed:` echoes the path exactly as given on the command line (relative stays
357	relative) `[R4 relative-path probe]`.
358	
359	## 11. Typed value validation (Round 4)
360	
361	Validation fires ONLY when a value string is present (via `=value`, `--oj=…`, or a
362	short-attached `-b5`); a BARE value flag (`--bound`, `--stop-on-trace`, …) never
363	errors `[R4 bare-vs-valued sweep]`. An empty `=` value IS a present value and is
364	validated (`--bound=` → error, `--stop-on-trace=` → `… method: `). All these errors
365	go to STDERR, exit 1, and use the app `error` shape
366	`tamarin-prover: <msg>\nCallStack (from HasCallStack):\n  error, called at
367	src/Main/Mode/Batch.hs:162:33 in main:Main.Mode.Batch`. In a normal run they follow
368	the maude preamble; under `--parse-only` no preamble precedes them.
369	
370	Integer flags → `<LABEL>: invalid bound given` `[R4 numeric sweep]`:
371	`--bound`→`bound`, `-c/--open-chains`→`OpenChainsLimit`,
372	`-s/--saturation`→`SaturationLimit`, `-d/--derivcheck-timeout`→`derivcheck-timeout`,
373	`--replication-bound`→`replication-bound`.
374	Accepted integer set = Haskell `readMaybe Int`: trims leading/trailing ASCII
375	whitespace, optional `-` (with optional whitespace before the digits), decimal /
376	`0x`hex / `0o`octal, silent overflow wrap; REJECTS `+`, `.`, `_`, trailing
377	non-whitespace, and empty. Negatives and 0 are accepted for ALL of them (the
378	"PositiveInteger" help annotation is not enforced at the CLI).
379	
380	Enum flags:
381	- `--stop-on-trace`: value lowercased, valid ∈ {dfs,bfs,seqdfs,sorry,none};
382	  invalid → `unknown stop-on-trace method: <lowercased-value>` (echoes value).
383	- `--partial-evaluation`: case-insensitive, valid ∈ {summary,verbose};
384	  invalid → `partial-evaluation: unknown option` (no echo).
385	- `--output-module` (`-m`): case-SENSITIVE, valid ∈
386	  {spthytyped,spthy,msr,proverifequiv,proverif,deepsec};
387	  invalid → `output mode not supported.` (no echo).
388	`--heuristic` is NOT validated (any chars accepted).
389	
390	Repeated valued flag → LAST occurrence wins / is the one validated
391	`[R4 repeated probe]`.
392	
393	Multi-invalid precedence is a FIXED order, independent of CLI arg order
394	`[R4 precedence sweep, all pairwise relations consistent]`:
395	`stop-on-trace > bound > partial-evaluation > open-chains > saturation >
396	output-module > derivcheck-timeout > replication-bound`.
397	
398	### 11a. Consumer-extension flags (NOT in the reference, NOT in help)
399	`--processors <positive int>`, `--maude-processes <positive int>`,
400	`--data-dir <path>` all yield `Unknown flag: --<name>` in the reference
401	`[R4 interop probe]`, confirming they are absent. The clean crate accepts them as
402	typed flags with its OWN validation (positive integer; any path); their rejection
403	texts are original (no reference to reproduce) and they are omitted from help.
404	
405	## 12. Summary body content (Round 5)  [r5_* captures]
406	
407	The `<result lines>` slot of §6/§10b (the summary-of-summaries body under the
408	`processing time:` line) has more structure than §6 recorded. Probed with
409	self-authored theories `probes/round5/*.spthy` (own fixtures, not the GPL
410	examples), split-stream, on both `--prove` and default runs.
411	
412	### 12a. Body layout — an opening line plus up to two sections
413	After the `  processing time: <T>s` line the body is, byte-exactly:
414	
415	    "  \n"                                   <- opening two-space line (ALWAYS present)
416	    <warning section, if any>                <- comes first
417	    "  \n" between the two sections          <- present iff BOTH sections present
418	    <lemma section, if any>
419	
420	i.e. an opening `  ` line, then the present sections joined by another `  ` line.
421	- No warnings, no lemmas → body is just `  \n` `[r5_nolemma_default]`.
422	- Lemmas only → `  \n` + lemma lines `[split_batch_default / r5_exists_verified]`.
423	- Warning only → `  \n` + warning section `[round2_fresh_public_constant / r5_freshpub_prove]`.
424	- Warning + lemmas → `  \n` + warning + `  \n` + lemmas `[r5_warn_lemma_*]`.
425	
426	### 12b. Warning section — one or two lines
427	    "  WARNING: <N> wellformedness check failed!\n"   (noun stays singular "check")
428	    "           The analysis results might be wrong!\n"   (SECOND line, conditional)
429	The advisory second line is left-padded 11 spaces — exactly the width of the
430	prefix `  WARNING: ` — so it aligns under the text after `WARNING: `. It is
431	emitted **iff the run is a proving run (`--prove`)**, independent of whether any
432	lemma was actually proved: a `--prove` run whose lemma prefix matches nothing
433	(all lemmas "analysis incomplete") still emits it `[r5_falsify_nomatch]`, and a
434	proving run with a warning but no lemmas emits it with no following separator
435	`[r5_freshpub_prove]`; a plain analyze run never does `[r5_warn_lemma_default]`.
436	A run with no warning never shows this line regardless of `--prove`
437	`[r5_nslpk3_prove_bound2, r5_nolemma_prove]`.
438	
439	### 12c. Lemma verdict lines — `  <name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)`
440	Observed `<verdict>` forms (the step count is the explored proof depth):
441	- `verified` — uniform for BOTH `all-traces` and `exists-trace`
442	  `[r5_exists_verified: can_ping/always_true both "verified"]`.
443	- `analysis incomplete` — default/unselected lemmas, AND `--prove` lemmas cut off
444	  by `--bound` (only the step count grows; NO extra line for the bound)
445	  `[r5_nslpk3_prove_bound2: nonce_secrecy "analysis incomplete (6 steps)"]`.
446	- `falsified` — carries a **kind-dependent suffix** `[r5_falsify_prove]`:
447	  - all-traces → `falsified - found trace` (a counter-example trace was produced),
448	  - exists-trace → `falsified - no trace found` (no witnessing trace exists).
449	  (Corrects the R2 guess that falsified prints as a bare `falsified`.)
450	
451	### 12d. Between-theory joining (multi-file)  [r5_multi_warn_prove]
452	Each theory's block is preceded by a single `\n` (blank line) before its
453	`analyzed:` header — unchanged from §10b; confirmed with a lemmas-only theory
454	followed by a warning+lemma theory. The per-theory warning/lemma structure of
455	§12a–c is entirely local to each block.
456	
457	Corresponding STDERR (unchanged framing, §10a): preamble once, then per theory
458	its five closed phases + any `[Saturating Sources] …` extra progress; the number
459	of saturating-sources lines is theory-specific (NSLPK3 emits four, the tiny
460	`WarnAndLemma`/`FreshPubConst` theories emit none) — treated as the opaque
461	per-theory `extra_progress` slot.
462	
463	## 13. Streaming emission contract (Round 5, INTEROP)
464	
465	The assembled framing of §6/§10 hands back complete `Streams` at the end. A
466	consumer that flushes output *as the prover runs* needs to drive emission
467	event-by-event. The clean crate adds an incremental surface (`src/emit.rs`) that
468	is a pure re-ordering of the SAME byte content — no new observable strings, so no
469	new oracle facts; it is an internal API shape, verified by equivalence against
470	the assembled model rather than against the oracle:
471	- `Sink::emit(stream, text)` — the consumer's byte destination; the consumer
472	  chooses flush timing (flush-per-call = true line streaming; buffering = coalesced).
473	- `BatchEmitter` — `begin` (preamble→stderr) / `progress`,`closed_phases`,
474	  `extra_progress` (→stderr) / `payload` (→stdout) / `record_summary` (buffered) /
475	  `finish` (combined summary→stdout). The summary is the one unavoidable trailing
476	  barrier (it spans every theory), matching the reference which prints it last.
477	- Equivalence property: for identical inputs the emitter's per-stream bytes equal
478	  `frame_batch`'s. The cross-stream interleaving differs in wall-clock time (the
479	  point of streaming) but each stream's byte sequence is identical, because on
480	  each stream both paths emit in the same order: stderr = preamble then per-theory
481	  progress; stdout = payloads in order then the single summary. Proven by tests
482	  `streaming_matches_assembled_on_shared_inputs` and
483	  `streaming_reproduces_captured_multifile_streams`.
484	
485	## 14. Completed lemma-verdict taxonomy (Round 6)  [r6_* captures]
486	
487	Round 5 recorded three regular-lemma verdict phrases. Round 6 drove every lemma
488	into every reachable terminal state to close the taxonomy. Probe theories are
489	self-authored in `probes/round6/*.spthy`; diff summary captures are copied to
490	`tests/fixtures/r6_diff_*.out.txt`.
491	
492	### 14a. The verdict-phrase set is exactly four (regular lemmas)
493	`<verdict>` in `<name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)` is one of:
494	- `verified` — uniform for both kinds (§12c).
495	- `falsified - found trace` — all-traces (§12c).
496	- `falsified - no trace found` — exists-trace (§12c).
497	- `analysis incomplete` — unselected, `--prove` pattern not matching (§12b/R5
498	  `r5_falsify_nomatch`), or cut off by `--bound` (§12c). No further phrase appears
499	  under `--stop-on-trace=SORRY|NONE` `[r6_sot_sorry, r6_sot_none]`, reloading a
500	  partially/fully embedded proof `[r6_analysed_reload: only verified/falsified]`,
501	  or for a `[sources]`-attributed lemma `[r6_partialdecon]`. The `[sources]`
502	  attribute is invisible in the summary — such a lemma prints with the identical
503	  line form (`secret (all-traces): falsified - found trace (4 steps)`).
504	
505	Lemma SELECTION never omits a line: a lemma the run did not prove — because
506	`--prove=PREFIX`/`--lemma=NAME` did not select it — still appears, as `analysis
507	incomplete`, never as a "skipped"/absent line `[r6_twolemma_proveone,
508	r6_twolemma_lemmafilter]`. `--prove=first` on a two-lemma theory prints `first`
509	verified and `second (exists-trace): analysis incomplete (1 steps)`. There is thus
510	no distinct summary form for an unselected/filtered lemma. A `--prove`/`--lemma`
511	PREFIX that matches no lemma is itself a wellformedness check ("... does not
512	correspond to a specified lemma in the theory") and so feeds the whole-theory
513	WARNING count-line slot (§12b), not a new per-lemma form `[r6_twolemma_lemmafilter:
514	count 1 + advisory]` — reinforcing §14e.
515	
516	Partial deconstructions / open chains do NOT add a summary-of-summaries line: a
517	theory that exercises them prints exactly one verdict line per lemma
518	`[r6_openchains_default, r6_woolam_noautosrc]` (they only add opaque stderr
519	`[Open Chains] …` progress and, when they trip a wellformedness rule, feed the
520	existing WARNING count-line slot).
521	
522	### 14b. `--diff` adds three prefixed line forms  [r6_diff_* captures]
523	Under `--diff` the summary body's lemma section gains side-prefixed lines. Byte
524	layout (each still carries the `  ` body indent of §12a):
525	
526	    "  RHS :  <name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)\n"   projected ordinary lemma
527	    "  LHS :  <name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)\n"   projected ordinary lemma
528	    "  DiffLemma:  <name> : <verdict> (<N> steps)\n"      observational equivalence
529	
530	- `RHS`/`LHS` prefix = `RHS` + ` :  ` (space, colon, two spaces); the remainder is
531	  the *identical* regular line form of §12c (the `<kind>` renders normally,
532	  including `exists-trace` `[r6_diff_two_lemmas: can_send]`).
533	  A projected line carries the SAME kind-dependent verdict set as a whole-theory
534	  lemma — earlier captures only exercised `verified`; the remaining projected
535	  verdicts were driven out explicitly:
536	  - projected falsified, both kinds: `RHS/LHS :  leaked (all-traces): falsified -
537	    found trace` and `RHS/LHS :  impossible (exists-trace): falsified - no trace
538	    found` `[r6_diff_false]`;
539	  - projected `analysis incomplete`: a `--diff` run WITHOUT `--prove` leaves each
540	    ordinary lemma's `RHS`/`LHS` lines (and the `DiffLemma`) at `analysis
541	    incomplete` `[r6_diff_lemma_noprove]`.
542	  The two sides are computed INDEPENDENTLY, not mirrored: when the two projected
543	  systems genuinely differ, one side's line can read `verified` while the other
544	  reads `falsified` for the same lemma (`RHS :  secret (all-traces): verified` vs
545	  `LHS :  secret (all-traces): falsified - found trace`) `[r6_diff_asym]`. `LHS`
546	  is the first `diff(L,R)` argument (the left system), `RHS` the second (the right
547	  system): here `diff(~m, senc(~m,~k))` gives LHS `Out(~m)` (secrecy false) and
548	  RHS `Out(senc(~m,~k))` (secrecy true).
549	- `DiffLemma` prefix = `DiffLemma` + `:  ` (colon, two spaces — NO space before the
550	  colon, unlike RHS/LHS). Its content is `<name> : <verdict>` (a single space each
551	  side of the colon) with NO `(<kind>)`: an observational-equivalence lemma has no
552	  trace-kind. Its `falsified` always reads `- found trace` (a distinguishing
553	  attack) `[r6_diff_n5n6: 8 steps; r6_diff_warn: 7 steps]`; `verified`
554	  `[r6_diff_equiv_true: 31 steps]`; `analysis incomplete` `[r6_diff_default]`.
555	- The auto-generated diff lemma is always named `Observational_equivalence`.
556	
557	### 14c. Diff summary ordering  [r6_diff_with_lemma, r6_diff_two_lemmas, r6_diff_warn]
558	Within one theory's body the lemma section is, in order:
559	1. for EACH ordinary lemma, its `RHS` line then its `LHS` line (the pair is
560	   grouped per lemma: `RHS l1, LHS l1, RHS l2, LHS l2, …`), then
561	2. the single `DiffLemma:` line, last.
562	The surrounding body structure of §12a is unchanged: opening `  ` line, then the
563	warning section first (with the §12b `--prove` advisory), then a `  ` separator,
564	then this lemma section `[r6_diff_warn]`.
565	
566	### 14d. Diff STDERR (documented, opaque — NOT byte-modelled)
567	Diff runs prefix the per-theory progress with a bare `diffLemma\n` line right
568	after the maude preamble, and the `[Saturating Sources] …` / `[Open Chains] …`
569	engine markers interleave *within* the five phases (not only after `Theory
570	closed`, unlike §10a) and in run-varying multiplicity `[r6_diff_* .err]`. Because
571	that interleaving is engine-timing-driven, the Round-6 fixtures assert only the
572	STDOUT summary section (via `render_summary`); diff stderr is treated as an opaque
573	progress stream, same policy as the `extra_progress` slot.
574	
575	### 14e. Lemma errors are NOT per-lemma verdicts
576	No probe produced an "error" verdict *line* in the summary. A malformed lemma
577	fails a wellformedness check → the whole-theory WARNING count-line slot (§12b), and
578	a solver/oracle failure aborts the entire run after `Theory closed` with a runtime
579	stderr line and empty stdout — e.g. a missing heuristic oracle:
580	`tamarin-prover: ./oracle: readCreateProcess: posix_spawnp: does not exist (No such
581	file or directory)` (exit 1) `[r6_oracle_err]`. These belong to the §8c runtime-error
582	surface, not the summary taxonomy.
583	
584	### 14f. Clean-crate model
585	`framing::LemmaOutcome` gains a `side: LemmaSide` field (`Whole` | `Rhs` | `Lhs` |
586	`Diff`); `Whole` keeps the pre-R6 rendering byte-for-byte. `Diff` renders the
587	`DiffLemma:` form and ignores `kind` (set to `AllTraces` by the `diff_lemma`
588	constructor). `Summary.lemmas` stays a single ordered `Vec` — a diff theory lists
589	its `Rhs`/`Lhs`/`Diff` entries in the §14c order and `render_block` prints them in
590	sequence. The verdict phrase moved to a shared `verdict_phrase(result, kind)`, so a
591	projected `Rhs`/`Lhs` line reuses it with that side's own `kind` — the projected
592	falsified/incomplete forms of the amended §14b are therefore rendered by the same
593	code path as whole-theory lemmas, now pinned against observed captures rather than
594	inferred. Byte-parity tests: `diff_summary_*` (eight captures: the original five
595	plus `r6_diff_false` projected-falsified-both-kinds, `r6_diff_lemma_noprove`
596	projected-incomplete, `r6_diff_asym` independent-sides) +
597	`diff_summary_line_bytes_are_exact`, and
598	`frame_batch_multi_warn_then_two_lemmas_reproduces_both_streams` re-verifies the
599	multi-lemma/multi-theory warning-vs-lemma interleaving (`r6_multi_warn_two`).

1	//! Output-stream model.
2	//!
3	//! Every byte the binary emits is destined for exactly one of the two standard
4	//! streams. Which stream a given piece of text lands on is a fixed, probed
5	//! property (see `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md` §10): the theory payload, the summary
6	//! block, help pages, the version banner, and the `error: … + full help`
7	//! validation envelopes go to stdout; the maude preamble, per-theory progress
8	//! markers, the bare cmdargs one-liners, and all runtime errors go to stderr.
9	
10	/// One of the two standard output streams.
11	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
12	pub enum Stream {
13	    /// Standard output.
14	    Out,
15	    /// Standard error.
16	    Err,
17	}
18	
19	/// A pair of independently byte-exact stream buffers.
20	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
21	pub struct Streams {
22	    pub out: String,
23	    pub err: String,
24	}
25	
26	impl Streams {
27	    /// Append `text` to the buffer for `stream`.
28	    pub fn push(&mut self, stream: Stream, text: &str) {
29	        match stream {
30	            Stream::Out => self.out.push_str(text),
31	            Stream::Err => self.err.push_str(text),
32	        }
33	    }
34	}

label="$1"; shift
timeout "${ORACLE_TIMEOUT:-120}" "$BIN" "$@" 1>"$CAP/$label.out.txt" 2>"$CAP/$label.err.txt"
rc=$?

echo "--- $label.out.txt ($(wc -c <"$CAP/$label.out.txt") bytes) ---"
cat "$CAP/$label.out.txt"
echo "--- $label.err.txt ($(wc -c <"$CAP/$label.err.txt") bytes) ---"
cat "$CAP/$label.err.txt"

===open_chains.spthy probe===
theory OpenChains
begin

builtins: symmetric-encryption

// The decryption key ~k lives only in the persistent !Key fact (never sent), so
// the source of the plaintext m produced by Decrypt cannot be refined by the
// adversary — this is expected to leave a PARTIAL DECONSTRUCTION (open chain).
rule CreateKey:
  [ Fr(~k) ] --> [ !Key(~k) ]

rule Encrypt:
  [ !Key(~k), In(m) ] --> [ Out(senc(m, ~k)) ]

rule Decrypt:
  [ !Key(~k), In(senc(m, ~k)) ] --[ Dec(m) ]-> [ Out(m) ]

lemma dec_secret:
  "All m #i. Dec(m) @ i ==> Ex #j. K(m) @ j"

end

===partialdecon.spthy probe===
theory PartialDecon
begin

builtins: symmetric-encryption

// A decryption rule whose key arrives from the network. The source of the
// plaintext m cannot be resolved, which typically leaves a partial
// deconstruction (open chain). Probe whether that surfaces in the summary.
rule Dec:
  [ In(senc(m, k)), In(k) ] --[ Got(m) ]-> [ Out(m) ]

lemma secret [sources]:
  "All m #i. Got(m) @ i ==> Ex #j. K(m) @ j"

end

=== FreshOrderingTest.spthy ===
theory FreshOrderingTest
begin

builtins: hashing

rule Start:
    [ Fr(~s) ]
  --[ Start(~s) ]->
    [ Starting(~s) ]

rule Start2:
    [ Starting(s) ]
  --[ Start2(s) ]->
    [ State(s, h(s)) ]

rule Step:
    [ State(s, x) ]
  --[ Step(s) ]->
    [ State(s, h(x))]

// this is directly solvable with the original freshOrdering rule
lemma Order:
	"All s #i #j. Start(s)@i ∧ Step(s)@j ⇒ i<j"

// this is directly solvable with the encanced freshOrdering rule but not with the original freshOrdering rule
lemma Order2:
	"All s #i #j. Start2(s)@i ∧ Step(s)@j ⇒ i<j"

end


=== YellowDiffTest.spthy ===
theory YellowDiffTest
begin

builtins: hashing

rule Start:
    [Fr(~s)]
  --[Start(~s, diff(fst(~s), <~s,~s>))]->
    [State(~s, diff(~s, <~s,~s>))]

rule End:
    [State(s, t)]
  --[End(s, t)]->
    [Out(s), Out(h(t))]

restriction Start2Complication:
"∀ s t #i. Start(s, t)@i ==> s ⊏ t"

lemma GreenYellow: exists-trace
"∃ s t #i. End(s, t)@i"

lemma RedYellow:
"¬∃ s t #i. End(s, t)@i"

lemma YellowRed: exists-trace
"¬∀ s t #i. End(s, t)@i ==> <s,s> = t"

lemma YellowGreen:
"∀ s t #i. End(s, t)@i ==> <s,s> = t"

end

## 15. A fourth non-diff verdict phrase (Round 7)  [r7_* captures]

Rounds 5–6 recorded the non-diff verdict set as exactly three phrases (verified /
falsified-{found trace|no trace found} / analysis incomplete). Round 7 found a
FOURTH, driven out with the GPL example `csf23-subterms/YellowTest.spthy`
(observation input only; a subterm theory whose proofs reach a terminal
`UNFINISHABLE` step). Probe theories self-authored in `probes/round7/*.spthy`;
split-stream captures via `probes/split_probe.sh` → `captures/r7_*.{out,err}.txt`,
copied to `tests/fixtures/`.

### 15a. `analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms)`
`--prove` on YellowTest reports two of its lemmas as
`[r7_yellow_prove]`:

    YellowRed (exists-trace): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (4 steps)
    YellowGreen (all-traces): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (4 steps)

(the other two lemmas of the same theory close normally: `GreenYellow
(exists-trace): verified (3 steps)` and `RedYellow (all-traces): falsified - found
trace (3 steps)`). Properties of this fourth phrase:
- It is UNIFORM across trace-kinds — both an `exists-trace` and an `all-traces`
  lemma print the identical string with NO falsified-style `- found trace` /
  `- no trace found` suffix (like `verified` and `analysis incomplete`, unlike
  `falsified`). The phrase is a fixed canned string; the parenthetical reason
  `(reducible operators in subterms)` did not vary across the observed lemmas
  (stays plural regardless of count, matching the singular-"check" convention of
  the WARNING count line, §12b). No OTHER `analysis cannot be finished (<reason>)`
  variant surfaced — the only feature that produced it in the whole example
  corpus is `csf23-subterms` (four files); FreshOrderingTest / ParserTests only
  ever `verified`, and it is the theory pretty-print's `by UNFINISHABLE //
  reducible operator in subterm` proof step surfacing in the summary.
- It carries NO accompanying warning or advisory: the YellowTest `--prove`
  summary has no WARNING section and no `The analysis results might be wrong!`
  line, and stderr is the plain 5 phases + `[Saturating Sources] Done`
  `[r7_yellow_prove.err]`. So this state does not feed the might-be-wrong advisory
  of §12b (that advisory tracks a wellformedness-check failure under `--prove`,
  not proof incompleteness).
- It is a PROVEN-terminal state: it appears only when the lemma is actually
  proved. A DEFAULT (no `--prove`) run of the same theory reports all four lemmas
  as `analysis incomplete (1 steps)` `[r7_yellow_default]` — the phrase requires
  the proof to run and hit the wall.

### 15b. Bound exhaustion is DISTINCT (still `analysis incomplete`)
`--prove --bound=2` on the SAME theory cuts every proof short BEFORE it reaches
the `UNFINISHABLE` step, so all four lemmas read `analysis incomplete (4 steps)`
`[r7_yellow_bound]` — never the reducible-operators phrase. This directly answers
the Round-7 question: a bounded/unfinished-by-bound analysis and a
reducible-operators wall are DIFFERENT verdicts even for the identical lemmas
(the bound one reuses the existing `analysis incomplete`, §12c). `--stop-on-trace=
SORRY` does NOT change the phrasing: it still shows the reducible-operators phrase
for the wall lemmas and verified/falsified for the rest `[r7_yellow_sotsorry]`
(consistent with §14a `r6_sot_*`).

### 15c. It composes with the `--diff` side prefixes and both kinds
`--diff --prove` on `csf23-subterms/YellowDiffTest.spthy` (whose `diff(...)`
makes only the LHS projection reducible) yields `[r7_yellowdiff_prove]`:

    RHS :  GreenYellow (exists-trace): verified (3 steps)
    LHS :  GreenYellow (exists-trace): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
    RHS :  RedYellow (all-traces): falsified - found trace (3 steps)
    LHS :  RedYellow (all-traces): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
    RHS :  YellowRed (exists-trace): falsified - no trace found (3 steps)
    LHS :  YellowRed (exists-trace): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
    RHS :  YellowGreen (all-traces): verified (3 steps)
    LHS :  YellowGreen (all-traces): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)
    DiffLemma:  Observational_equivalence : falsified - found trace (9 steps)

So the phrase is rendered by the SAME shared verdict path (§14f `verdict_phrase`)
as a whole-theory lemma: it slots behind the `RHS :  `/`LHS :  ` prefix
unchanged, for both trace-kinds, exactly like every other verdict; the sides are
independent (RHS closes, LHS hits the wall).

### 15d. Other candidate states re-confirmed to add NO new phrase
- `[sources]`/typing lemmas: with `--auto-sources`, the auto-generated
  `AUTO_typing (all-traces): verified (12 steps)` renders as a plain lemma line —
  no special "sources" summary line `[r7_openchains_autosrc]`. A theory whose
  partial deconstructions never resolve just proof-searches forever under a plain
  `--prove` (killed by timeout, exit 124, empty stdout — a resource condition, not
  a summary phrase) `[r7_openchains_prove]`. Confirms §14a: partial
  deconstructions / open chains add no summary-of-summaries line.
- Induction: a lemma marked `[use_induction]` (exists-trace or all-traces) proves
  normally to `verified`/`falsified` with no distinct phrase and no error
  `[r7_induction_prove]`.
- Lemma errors / solver failures / `--prove=<pattern>` non-match: unchanged from
  §14e / §12b — they abort with a runtime stderr line or feed the WARNING
  count-line slot, never a per-lemma verdict.

### 15e. Clean-crate model
`framing::LemmaResult` gains a fourth variant `AnalysisCannotBeFinished`;
`verdict_phrase` maps it to the canned
`analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms)` for both kinds, so
`Whole`/`Rhs`/`Lhs`/`Diff` rendering picks it up automatically (no per-side code).

(split-stream, both kinds, `r7_yellow_prove`),
`summary_bound_exhaustion_is_incomplete_not_unfinishable` (the distinct bound case,
`r7_yellow_bound`), `diff_summary_projected_reducible_operators_both_sides_and_kinds`
(`r7_yellowdiff_prove`), and `reducible_operators_line_bytes_are_exact` (capture-
independent phrase bytes, whole + projected). All prior fixtures/tests stay green
(59 total).
